decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly summon a horde of goblins onto the battlefield and overwhelm the opponent with their sheer numbers and aggressive abilities. By utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey to put goblins into play for free, and cards like Goblin Warchief to reduce the cost of goblin spells, the deck aims to swarm the opponent with powerful creatures like Goblin Piledriver and Siege-Gang Commander. Additional cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Goblin Sharpshooter provide removal options to clear the way for the goblins to attack relentlessly.

The focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing efficient creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic, along with direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate. The use of efficient burn spells and creatures that can quickly deal damage helps to maintain pressure on the opponent and control the pace of the game. This deck aims to close out the game quickly, before the opponent has a chance to stabilize and mount a defense.

The Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and disrupting their hand with spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress. The deck aims to control the board with efficient removal like Smother and Withered Wretch, while generating card advantage with spells like Phyrexian Arena and Graveborn Muse. By applying pressure early in the game with creatures such as Nantuko Shade and Hypnotic Specter, the deck aims to maintain tempo and force the opponent to react defensively, ultimately leading to victory through attrition.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana with elves and ramping into powerful threats like Multani's Acolyte and Deranged Hermit. Survival of the Fittest helps tutor creatures, while Wirewood Symbiote provides protection and recursion. Tangle Wire can disrupt opponents while Priest of Titania adds to the mana pool. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast, aggressive strategy fueled by the synergy between elves and powerful creatures.

This Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on using efficient and aggressive creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Knight of Stromgald to quickly apply pressure to the opponent. It also includes disruption cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan and clear the path for attacking. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, with cards like Mishra's Factory and Dark Ritual providing additional mana acceleration. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early threats and disruption while maintaining a solid creature base to close out the game quickly.

The focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells in order to gain a quick advantage and maintain control over the game. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual and Duress to accelerate mana and disrupt the opponent's strategy, along with efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade, the deck aims to consistently apply pressure while also disrupting the opponent's hand and board presence. This aggressive mono black strategy is designed to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in a premodern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck revolves around assembling the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to quickly and efficiently win the game. The deck includes a lot of card draw and cantrips to help find the combo pieces quickly, along with counter spells to protect the combo from disruption. The goal is to use Stifle to counter the Dreadnought's ability to sacrifice itself, allowing the player to have a 12/12 creature on the board for only one mana. This combo can often catch opponents off guard and win the game in a single turn.

The main focus of this RG Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive creatures and burn spells. By using cards like Survival of the Fittest to search for the perfect creatures and Incinerate and Lightning Bolt to deal direct damage to the opponent or remove threats, this deck aims to control the board and finish the game quickly. With efficient creatures like Basking Rootwalla and Wild Mongrel combined with powerful support cards like Call of the Herd, this deck is designed to apply constant pressure and secure a swift victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through various spells and creatures, with a particular emphasis on disruption and protection. With cards like Meddling Mage and Counter spells like Mana Leak and Memory Lapse, the deck aims to prevent the opponent from executing their game plan effectively. In addition, cards like Standstill and Fact or Fiction provide card advantage and allow for strategic decision-making. The inclusion of cards like Swords to Plowshares and Exalted Angel offer efficient removal and win conditions respectively, making this a well-rounded control deck.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with Mox Diamond and Gemstone Mine to play powerful threats such as Wild Mongrel and Terravore. It then looks to disrupt the opponent's resources with cards like Armageddon and Wasteland, while maintaining card advantage and consistency through Sylvan Library. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and land destruction, ultimately controlling the game and securing a victory.

This deck focuses on assembling the Dream Halls combo as quickly as possible by using card draw spells like Opt, Impulse, and Prophetic Bolt. The goal is to cast a Show and Tell or Cunning Wish to put Dream Halls into play, then use it to discard cards like Fire/Ice or Prophetic Bolt to enable powerful effects. Seismic Assault serves as a backup win condition by dealing damage directly to the opponent. Counterspells and bounce lands help protect the combo and ensure a smooth game plan.

The deck revolves around assembling the combo pieces needed to generate a large storm count and mill the opponent's library with Brain Freeze. Cards like Intuition, Ill-Gotten Gains, and Recoup are used to find and recur important pieces of the combo. The deck also includes various mana acceleration cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to facilitate the combo quickly. The use of Gemstone Mine and City of Brass allows for multiple color options to cast the necessary spells. Overall, the strategy focuses on quickly assembling the combo and overwhelming the opponent with a large storm count.

The main concept of this deck revolves around utilizing the Terravore creature, which gains power and toughness for each land card in all graveyards. The deck focuses on aggressive plays with cards like Wild Mongrel and Treetop Village, while also incorporating land destruction elements with cards like Armageddon and Wasteland. Additionally, the deck includes card draw and removal spells to control the game and maintain board presence. Ultimately, the goal is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with large, land-powered creatures while disrupting their game plan with resource denial tactics.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo pieces needed to generate a large storm count, using Ill-Gotten Gains to recurse key cards like Brain Freeze and Dark Ritual. The deck aims to generate a high storm count to cast Brain Freeze and mill the opponent out in one turn. The deck is designed to be fast and efficient, utilizing cards like Lion's Eye Diamond and Lotus Petal to generate mana quickly and Intuition to search for key combo pieces. The deck is heavily reliant on graveyard recursion and careful sequencing of spells to win the game.

This deck focuses on assembling the Tinker combo, which involves using Tinker to search for and put Phyrexian Devourer onto the battlefield, then sacrificing it to Fling to deal a massive amount of damage to the opponent. The deck includes various mana sources and card draw spells to help assemble the combo quickly, as well as protection in the form of Defense Grid. The goal is to win the game quickly by taking advantage of the synergy between Tinker and Fling.

The main concept of this Combo - Full English Breakfast deck is to utilize the Survival of the Fittest card to quickly search for and set up a combo involving cards like Phyrexian Devourer, Triskelion, and Wall of Roots. The deck focuses on quickly assembling these pieces to create a powerful win condition, often through infinite damage or mill. The inclusion of cards like Impulse, Intuition, and Mana Leak help to set up and protect the combo, while cards like Birds of Paradise and Krosan Tusker provide ramp and consistency.
